description:
You are a QA engineer who has just received a small sample data-set for a
    new user-management micro-service.  
    A CSV file already exists at:

        /home/user/project/data/users.csv

    Your assignment consists of three very small steps.

    1. Filter the file so that **only** the records whose “status” column is
       literally “active” remain.  
       • Preserve the original header line.  
       • Save the result to a *new* file named

            /home/user/project/data/active_users.csv

    2. Convert that new CSV into JSON.  
       • The JSON must be an array of objects.  
       • Each object’s keys must be the exact CSV header names
         (id, name, email, status) in that order.  
       • Use two-space indentation.  
       • Save the output to

            /home/user/project/data/active_users.json

    3. Finally, append a short, human-readable log entry that confirms the
       operation.  
       • The log file must be

            /home/user/project/logs/process.log

       • If the file or its parent directory does not exist, create them.  
       • Append **exactly** the following three lines (no extra spaces or blank
         lines, letter-case must match, a trailing newline **is** required):

            active_users.csv rows: 2
            active_users.json objects: 2
            DONE

    Summary of files that must exist when you are finished:
       /home/user/project/data/active_users.csv    (with 3 lines total)
       /home/user/project/data/active_users.json   (pretty-printed array)
       /home/user/project/logs/process.log         (contains the 3 log lines)

    No other files should be created or modified.
truth:
Input file that is already present before the task starts:
        Path: /home/user/project/data/users.csv
        Content (4 lines total, *including* the header):
            id,name,email,status
            1,John Doe,john@example.com,active
            2,Jane Smith,jane@sample.com,inactive
            3,Bob Lee,bob@domain.com,active
            4,Alice Kay,alice@website.org,pending

    Expected file #1 (must be produced by the agent):
        Path: /home/user/project/data/active_users.csv
        Content (3 lines total, newline at the end of each line):
            id,name,email,status
            1,John Doe,john@example.com,active
            3,Bob Lee,bob@domain.com,active

    Expected file #2 (must be produced by the agent):
        Path: /home/user/project/data/active_users.json
        Exact content (including spaces, newlines, trailing newline):
            [
              {
                "id": "1",
                "name": "John Doe",
                "email": "john@example.com",
                "status": "active"
              },
              {
                "id": "3",
                "name": "Bob Lee",
                "email": "bob@domain.com",
                "status": "active"
              }
            ]

    Expected file #3 (must be produced/updated by the agent):
        Path: /home/user/project/logs/process.log
        Exact content (3 lines, final newline required):
            active_users.csv rows: 2
            active_users.json objects: 2
            DONE

    No additional files or directories should be left behind.
